items.prompt: What distinguishes a conditional GAN (cGAN) from a basic GAN?
choices: Generator and discriminator are conditioned on labels or attributes
It only works on financial time series
items.explain: Section 10.4.3 describes cGANs as conditioning both networks on additional information such as class labels.

items.prompt: CycleGAN is designed for image-to-image translation when paired training examples are unavailable.
items.explain: Section 10.4.3 introduces CycleGAN for unpaired domain translation with cycle consistency.

choices: Similarity between feature distributions of real and generated images
items.explain: Section 10.4.6 defines FID as comparing feature distributions of real and generated images.

items.prompt: Name one ethical misuse risk highlighted for GANs in this chapter.
items.rubric: Mention deepfakes, forged identity documents, fake news, or fraudulent synthetic traces.
items.sample_answer: Deepfakes—synthetic media that impersonates people or fabricates events—are a prominent GAN misuse risk.

items.prompt: Example 10.11 shows label-conditioned generation of categories such as cats, dogs, or cars.
items.explain: Example 10.11 describes a conditional GAN generating class-specific synthetic images from provided labels.
